column2: A user connects a phone to a computer with a cable and expects more than one task to happen. Name two common purposes that mobile-device connections can support.
column3: Common purposes include charging, data synchronization, file transfer, backup, and device identification.
column4:
Why

Mobile-device connections are not limited to one function. A single connection may provide power and data movement at the same time.

Exam Trap

Do not treat “connecting a mobile device” as only a charging topic; the objective also includes data and identification use cases.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2

column2: A technician finds an older mobile device with a connector that is larger than Micro-USB but still smaller than full-size USB. Which older connector type is most likely?
column4:
Why

Mini-USB is an older mobile-device connector and is larger than Micro-USB. Micro-USB became more common on many mobile devices before USB-C adoption.

Tech Wisdom

Connector age and size are not the same as data capability. Identify the physical connector first, then verify what the device supports.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2

column2: A tablet has a USB-C port, and the user wants to connect it to an external display through a USB-C adapter. What should you verify before assuming the adapter will work?
column3: Verify that the device and adapter support the needed display signal, not just the USB-C connector shape.
column4:
Why

USB-C describes the reversible physical connector. The same connector can carry different supported signals depending on the device and adapter.

Exam Trap

Do not assume every USB-C port supports DisplayPort, HDMI, Thunderbolt, or every USB data mode.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic HighYield Scenario Connector USB

column2: A company wants users to tap a phone or badge near a workstation reader for quick authentication. Which mobile-device connection method best fits this requirement?
column4:
Why

NFC is designed for very short-range wireless exchange, which makes it useful for tap-style payment, identity, and access-token workflows.

Exam Trap

NFC is not the best answer for an ongoing headset or speaker connection. Use Bluetooth for that type of peripheral.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic HighYield Scenario NFC Authentication

column2: A user wants a phone to connect wirelessly to headphones and a smartwatch over short distances. Which technology should they use?
column4:
Why

Bluetooth is used for short-range personal-area connections between mobile devices and nearby peripherals.

Related Cards

1.2-B005 tests NFC selection for tap-style workflows. This card tests ongoing peripheral connectivity.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic HighYield Scenario Bluetooth Peripheral

column2: A laptop has no available Wi-Fi network, but the user's phone has cellular data. What mobile feature can share the phone's Internet connection with the laptop, and what should you check before relying on it?
column3: Use hotspot/tethering, and check whether the phone and cellular plan allow it, including possible data limits or charges.
column4:
Why

Hotspot and tethering features let the phone extend its cellular data connection to other devices.

Real Help Desk

If the feature is missing or blocked, the issue may be the carrier plan or provider policy rather than the laptop.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic HighYield Scenario Tethering Troubleshooting

column2: A user buys a digital stylus for a tablet, but pressure-sensitive drawing and button features do not work. What is the best first thing to verify?
column3: Verify that the stylus is compatible with that tablet model or device family.
column4:
Why

Advanced stylus features depend on direct communication between the stylus and the device. A generic stylus may not support device-specific features.

Exam Trap

Do not assume any pointed input tool is equivalent to an active stylus.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic Scenario Stylus Input

column2: A user needs hands-free audio with both earphones and a microphone. Name two supported headset connection options from Objective 1.2.
column3: Examples include Bluetooth, USB, a 3.5 mm TRRS analog connector, or a compatible device-specific connector.
column4:
Why

Headsets combine listening and microphone input. The connection choice depends on the device ports and whether the user needs wired or wireless operation.

Exam Trap

Standard stereo headphones and a headset are not always the same thing; the microphone path matters.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic Scenario Audio Accessory

column2: A user wants a desktop-style setup with external keyboard, mouse, and additional interfaces. Which accessory is the better fit: a docking station or a port replicator?
column3: A docking station is the better fit when the user needs a broader desktop-style expansion setup.
column4:
Why

Docking stations extend existing interfaces and can add functionality. Port replicators are similar but are usually simpler and commonly connect over USB.

Exam Trap

If the question emphasizes expansion-card capability, the approved source points away from a basic port replicator.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2

column2: A user keeps accidentally moving the pointer while typing on a laptop. Which built-in input device setting should you consider disabling or adjusting?
column4:
Why

Trackpads can replace a mouse, but accidental taps or pointer movement can interfere with typing.

Real Help Desk

This is often a usability issue, not a hardware failure. Check the trackpad settings before replacing parts.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic Scenario Trackpad Input

column2: A desktop user needs real-time video for meetings. Which accessory category from Objective 1.2 commonly provides this capability?
column4:
Why

Cameras and webcams are used by video conferencing, photo, and multimedia applications. Desktops commonly use external webcams, while many mobile devices include internal cameras.

Source Note

This card stays at the accessory-role level and does not make claims about specific camera models or resolutions.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic Scenario Camera Accessory

column2: A designer needs precise pen input on a workstation, but the display is not a touchscreen. Which mobile-device accessory best fits this requirement?
column3: A drawing pad with an active stylus and external digitizer.
column4:
Why

A drawing pad provides a separate digitizer surface for precise stylus input. That makes it different from tapping directly on a touchscreen or using a trackpad for pointer movement.

Exam Trap

Do not answer with “stylus” by itself when the scenario requires an external input surface. The drawing pad is the accessory; the stylus is the tool used with it.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.3; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.2
column9: A+::220-1201::1.2 A+::220-1201::ConnectingMobileDevices Basic Scenario DrawingPad Input
